title: The Hyuga Aida connection
wikitext:
[]: The Hyuga-Aida connection?             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Sep 23, 2006 7:20 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: The basis of Reichu's theory, like most in Evangelion, is the principle that recurring elements and themes in the show represent connections and relations between characters and events. Repetition of any kind in the show is usually taken to be a subtle hint by the director for us to compare and contrast often temporally distant scenes. In Mr. Tines' words; "Once is happenstance, twice is coincidence, and three times is enemy actionâ€¦". This may at first seem a little flimsy, but indepth study of some of these repititions connections can be very persuasive. It's clear that the director does use this type of narritive technique a lot. "Transitivity" is not so much preferred as is general congruity of a theory with the overall plot. The trouble is Evangelion is a rather intricate tapestry, and every thread can probably find a connection to another by some convoluted path, so you have to have a fairly solid stack of connections to back your theory up.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sun Sep 24, 2006 5:34 am REICHU WANNABE!!! sry jk.. :oops:

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Sep 24, 2006 7:05 am -The characters look very similar, i.e. both wear the same style of glasses, same hair color, etc.

[]: Re: The Hygua-Aida connection?             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Sep 24, 2006 7:12 am CorporalChaos wrote: -Both have a thing for Misato.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 24, 2006 8:38 am master0rolando wrote: REICHU WANNABE!!!

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Sep 24, 2006 10:20 am Reichu wrote: the seventh child wrote: And i don't get it, why does every character must connected with someone other? I mean, if you look close enough, you'll always find similarities, but this doesn't mean they're connected!

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Sun Sep 24, 2006 10:41 am The Pen^2-Gendo connection: -Both are quiet and introverted. -Both have something to do with the South Pole. -Both are actually planning much more than it seems. -And we all remember that Pen-Pen's backpack actually contains Gendo's face. MORE TO COME (I don't see how Kensuke and Makoto relate (other than the fact that NO ONE LIKES EITHER OF THEM ), but I understand that this is simply an exercise in analysis and uber-fan-wankery. That said, I look forward to seeing how far you stretch this. Also, both characters are almost completely non-essential to the series. Kensuke exists only as an ironic counterpoint to the reluctant pilot Toji (otherwise he would just be another random classmate; you could have substituted any of them for him in the scene in the field in Ep. 4), and Makoto exists only to help Misato out at various points. Any of the other techs could have done this, but it's more fitting for him since he is in love with her. Oh well, c'est la vie.)

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Sep 24, 2006 11:44 am Reichu wrote: master0rolando wrote: REICHU WANNABE!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 24, 2006 1:19 pm Shin-seiki wrote: So, you wannabe an overaged anime nerd with no life...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Sep 24, 2006 1:51 pm This is all on your own head Reichu. This and all else after!

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Sep 24, 2006 8:47 pm Oh crap! Look what youve done! Now people are going to start speculating about how Penpen is actually related to keel somehow because lets face it they kind of look like each other :o

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 24, 2006 8:49 pm Hold on another day, I'm still looking for more supporting arguements, plus my computer isn't working too well with this stuff. I'll get it up soon enough.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 24, 2006 9:56 pm Defectron wrote: Oh crap! Look what youve done! Now people are going to start speculating about how Penpen is actually related to keel somehow because lets face it they kind of look like each other :o

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Sep 26, 2006 9:26 pm Sorry to keep you all waiting. My first piece of evidence. Episode 17 Kensuke: Well, what about the story that Unit-04 was lost? Shinji: Was...lost? Kensuke: You really don't know?!? It disappeared along with the entire Second Branch! My dad's whole section was in a panic! Shinji: Are-Are you sure...? Kensuke: I've got my sources.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 26, 2006 9:35 pm ^ That's one of the items I anticipated.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Sep 26, 2006 9:44 pm I thought it would be.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Sep 27, 2006 3:21 pm You have a lot of work ahead of you if that was your largest piece of evidence.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 27, 2006 5:49 pm In fact, I realized that the "second" thing I had in mind wasn't actually relevant at all. (My memory had played tricks on me.) Whoops.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Sep 28, 2006 4:03 pm I sense... yes It's clear now...yes... they both... they both ... Yes! Unrequited love for Misato! There, the circle is complete.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Sep 29, 2006 5:55 am So the largest piece of evidence is that they both used the line "i've got my sources".. Sorry, doesn't make sense to me. i mean, when you give someone information to someone about something and then he asks you " are you sure?", the logic thing to answer is " don't worry, i've got my sources", or something like that! Doesn't seem any connection here.

Repetition of any kind in the show is usually taken to be a subtle hint by the director for us to compare and contrast often temporally distant scenes. In Mr. Tines' words; "Once is happenstance, twice is coincidence, and three times is enemy actionâ€¦". This may at first seem a little flimsy, but indepth study of some of these repititions connections can be very persuasive. It's clear that the director does use this type of narritive technique a lot. "Transitivity" is not so much preferred as is general congruity of a theory with the overall plot. The trouble is Evangelion is a rather intricate tapestry, and every thread can probably find a connection to another by some convoluted path, so you have to have a fairly solid stack of connections to back your theory up.
